us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic and U.S.-policy stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Trump meets NATO leaders in Ankara as allies pitch higher defense spending
bullets: President Trump met NATO leaders Tuesday at a two-day summit in Ankara, Turkey.
Allies are showcasing military projects worth billions of dollars to show stronger European defense commitments.
The summit follows Trump’s repeated criticism of NATO burden-sharing and U.S. troop commitments in Europe.

articles.title: Trump renews warning to Iran, saying U.S. will make a deal or “finish the job”
bullets: Trump said Monday the U.S. would either reach a deal with Iran or take further military action.
Indirect U.S.-Iran talks ended last week without public signs of a lasting agreement.
The comments came after Iran held funeral ceremonies for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

articles.title: Fourth of July weekend gun violence leaves dozens dead across the U.S.
bullets: The Guardian reported at least 43 deaths during the holiday weekend’s gun violence.
Shootings were reported in multiple cities, including Los Angeles, Austin, Augusta and Chicago.
The violence marred celebrations of the United States’ 250th anniversary.

articles.title: Deadly heat and storms disrupt July Fourth events across several states
bullets: CBS/AP reported at least 25 suspected heat-related deaths in New Jersey.
Extreme heat and severe storms disrupted events including National Mall celebrations in Washington.
Forecasters warned millions about flooding risks after the holiday weekend.

articles.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs as Xbox and commercial units restructure
bullets: Microsoft said it is cutting about 2.1% of its global workforce.
The reductions affect commercial operations and Xbox, with 1,600 Xbox jobs cut immediately.
The company said AI is changing work patterns but said affected roles are not simply being replaced by AI.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Major worldwide stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: NATO summit opens in Turkey with focus on defense spending and Ukraine
bullets: NATO leaders gathered in Ankara for a two-day summit beginning Tuesday.
European allies are highlighting new military projects to show they can shoulder more defense responsibility.
Trump is also expected to meet Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy during the summit.

articles.title: China’s submarine-launched missile test raises alarm across the Pacific
bullets: China test-fired a long-range ballistic missile from a nuclear-powered submarine Monday.
The missile carried a dummy warhead and landed in the South Pacific, according to official Chinese media.
Japan, Australia, New Zealand, Taiwan and the U.S. raised concerns about the launch.

articles.title: Cargo ship attacked off Yemen as Red Sea security worries return
bullets: A bulk carrier reported being attacked southwest of Hodeida, Yemen, on Sunday.
UK maritime authorities said the ship and crew were safe after armed assailants approached by skiff.
No group immediately claimed responsibility, though Houthi threats have renewed shipping concerns.

articles.title: Australia and Fiji sign mutual defense pact amid Pacific security tensions
bullets: Australia and Fiji sealed a new mutual defense agreement Monday.
The pact is part of Australia’s push to deepen Pacific security ties and counter China’s influence.
The announcement coincided with China’s missile test in the South Pacific.

articles.title: NATO and Gulf Arab ministers discuss Strait of Hormuz tensions
bullets: NATO foreign ministers met Gulf Arab counterparts Tuesday on the sidelines of the Ankara summit.
Talks focused on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and a proposed Franco-British maritime mission.
Shipping disruption remains a pressure point for oil prices and global supply chains.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India-focused stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: India and Indonesia seal BrahMos and Astra missile deals
bullets: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto concluded major defense agreements Tuesday.
India will supply BrahMos cruise missiles and Astra air-to-air missiles to Indonesia.
The deals were part of a broader push to deepen Indo-Pacific strategic cooperation.

articles.title: Wayanad landslide kills at least two as north Kerala faces heavy rain alerts
bullets: A landslide hit the Kalladi tunnel project site in Kerala’s Wayanad district Tuesday.
Hindustan Times reported at least two deaths and several people missing in live updates.
IMD issued red alerts for Wayanad and Kozhikode, with extremely heavy rain expected in north Kerala.

articles.title: Mumbai schools shut and expressway section reopens after heavy rain
bullets: Mumbai schools and colleges were closed Tuesday after an IMD orange alert for heavy rain and winds.
The Mumbai-Pune Expressway Missing Link reopened after a landslide-related closure of nearly 19 hours.
Authorities also warned tourists to avoid parts of Nashik amid a cloudburst warning.

articles.title: Ram Temple Trust accepts key resignations amid donation irregularity allegations
bullets: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust accepted Champat Rai’s resignation as general secretary.
Trustee Anil Mishra also resigned, and administrator Gopal Rao was removed.
The moves follow allegations of irregularities involving temple donations that surfaced last month.

articles.title: Kharif sowing improves as July rains narrow India’s monsoon deficit
bullets: Agriculture ministry data showed Kharif acreage at 44.3 million hectares as of July 5.
The weekly acreage deficit narrowed to 20.8% from 22.7% the prior week.
A stronger July monsoon helped reduce the countrywide rainfall deficit after a dry June.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Top U.S.-listed market movers and stock catalysts

articles.title: Broadcom rallies after extending Apple chip partnership through 2031
bullets: Broadcom said it will expand its Apple chip supply partnership through 2031.
Reuters reported Broadcom shares rose nearly 4% in premarket trading after the announcement.
The deal covers custom chips used across multiple Apple product generations.

articles.title: Crinetics surges after Vertex announces $10 billion takeover
bullets: Vertex Pharmaceuticals agreed to buy Crinetics Pharmaceuticals for about $10 billion.
TipRanks listed Crinetics as the top premarket gainer Tuesday, up nearly 99%.
The deal made Crinetics one of the day’s biggest single-stock movers.

articles.title: AI chip rally lifts Tesla, AMD and Dell
bullets: Tesla and AMD each rose more than 6% Monday as AI-linked shares rebounded.
Dell gained more than 4% after President Trump publicly endorsed buying Dell computers.
The SOXX semiconductor ETF advanced 2.68%, recovering part of the prior week’s slide.

articles.title: Memory and storage stocks jump in semiconductor rebound
bullets: Western Digital rose more than 9% and Seagate gained nearly 6% in Monday’s chip rally.
KuCoin cited broad gains across AMD, Broadcom, Arm, Qualcomm, TSMC, Intel and Marvell.
The rebound followed renewed investor interest in AI and memory-chip demand.

articles.title: Microsoft shares slip after 4,800 job-cut plan
bullets: Microsoft stock fell after the company announced workforce reductions of about 2.1%.
The cuts focus on commercial operations and Xbox restructuring.
The move came as investors weighed heavy AI spending against cost controls.

business.label: Business
business.subtitle: Major corporate and deal stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Microsoft launches major Xbox overhaul during companywide layoffs
bullets: Microsoft is cutting 4,800 jobs across its global workforce.
Xbox will lose 1,600 roles immediately, with more reductions expected during fiscal 2027.
The company is restructuring gaming and commercial operations to improve focus and profitability.

articles.title: Vertex to acquire Crinetics in $10 billion pharmaceutical deal
bullets: Vertex Pharmaceuticals agreed to buy Crinetics Pharmaceuticals for about $10 billion in equity value.
The companies announced the deal Monday.
Crinetics shares jumped sharply after the takeover news.

articles.title: Apple secures long-term Broadcom chip supply through 2031
bullets: Broadcom will continue developing and supplying custom chips for Apple under a new multi-year agreement.
Analysts estimate Apple accounts for about 20% of Broadcom’s annual revenue.
The arrangement strengthens Apple’s supply-chain planning for wireless and radio-frequency components.

articles.title: SK Hynix launches massive U.S. listing to fund AI memory expansion
bullets: SK Hynix is launching an approximately $28 billion U.S. listing, according to Reuters.
The offering is among the world’s largest new share sales.
The South Korean memory-chip maker is seeking to capitalize on investor demand tied to AI infrastructure.

articles.title: Samsung forecasts 19-fold jump in quarterly operating profit
bullets: Samsung Electronics forecast a 1,810% year-over-year rise in second-quarter operating profit.
Reuters said AI-driven demand continued to lift memory-chip prices.
The preliminary result beat expectations but came amid market debate over the durability of the AI rally.

tech.label: Tech
tech.subtitle: Top technology stories and platform developments

articles.title: Samsung’s AI memory boom drives record profit guidance
bullets: Samsung projected second-quarter operating profit of about 89.4 trillion won.
Reuters said the result would mark a third straight quarter of record operating profit.
Strong AI memory demand and higher chip prices powered the surge.

articles.title: Researchers report first documented agentic ransomware operation
bullets: Sysdig researchers said an AI agent called JadePuffer carried out an end-to-end ransomware intrusion.
The agent exploited a vulnerable Langflow server, harvested credentials and moved laterally through systems.
TechCrunch reported a human was still involved outside the technical execution of the attack.

articles.title: Nintendo to stop selling original Switch models in Europe in 2027
bullets: Nintendo plans to stop selling Switch, Switch Lite and Switch OLED hardware in Europe after mid-February 2027.
The move is tied to European battery rules requiring user-replaceable batteries for certain devices.
Existing consoles will continue working, and the decision has not been announced for North America.

articles.title: Apple and Broadcom extend custom-chip partnership to 2031
bullets: Broadcom will supply Apple with custom chips under expanded long-term agreements.
The partnership includes components such as radio-frequency, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth and networking semiconductors.
The deal supports Apple’s supply-chain resilience even as it designs more chips in-house.

articles.title: Microsoft cuts nearly 5,000 roles as AI-era restructuring continues
bullets: Microsoft eliminated about 4,800 roles across Xbox, commercial sales and other operations.
TechCrunch reported Xbox will cut 1,600 workers immediately and about 3,200 through fiscal 2027.
The layoffs coincide with Microsoft’s increased spending on AI deployment and customer engineering.
